cases.label: happy_path

input.strategy_route: Promote an inn in an old town for couples seeking a one-night or two-night slow stay using courtyard atmosphere, evening walk rhythm, breakfast texture, and a consultative room-matching CTA
input.offer_info: courtyard rooms and selected view rooms, simple breakfast, evening tea or shared sitting area, walkable old-town lanes, some rooms differ by floor and quietness, no guaranteed river view or zero-noise claims
input.audience_segment: couples from nearby cities who want a more memorable overnight stay than a standardized hotel and care about atmosphere from evening through next morning

mustInclude: opening anchored to slowing down in the destination rather than just booking a room
specific proof around courtyard or sitting space, evening strolling rhythm, breakfast or next-morning experience, and room-type difference
CTA that asks for dates, number of nights, room preference, and whether the stay is for a celebration or quiet reset
mustAvoid: claiming every room has the same view or same quietness
hard urgency that pushes instant booking without confirming dates

cases.label: happy_path

input.strategy_route: Drive inquiries for an inn suited to friends planning a short gathering by highlighting courtyard hangout value, local route suggestions, and low-pressure consultation
input.offer_info: small number of rooms, public courtyard or rooftop seating, local food and walking recommendations, possible parking guidance, clear check-in boundaries, no unrestricted party-style overpromises
input.audience_segment: friends or small groups comparing distinctive stays for birthdays, reunion weekends, or old-town catch-up trips

mustInclude: opening tied to catching up, slowing down, or spending the evening together after walking around town
trust modules around room count, public-space use, local route recommendations, and arrival or parking logic
next step asking for dates, guest count, room count, and whether courtyard time, celebration setup, or parking support matters most
mustAvoid: pretending the whole inn is always fully private without confirming booking scope
language that implies unrestricted nightlife or party use

cases.label: edge_case

input.strategy_route: Write inn copy for a vague weekend inquiry that only says it wants an atmospheric place, while dates, guest count, transport mode, luggage situation, and room preference are still unclear
input.offer_info: mixed room styles, some stairs, possible stone-lane walk after parking, limited inventory, uncertain breakfast inclusion, no confirmed room assignment yet
input.audience_segment: a user comparing several old-town or mountain inns but unable to explain whether they care more about quietness, view, access convenience, or public-space atmosphere

mustInclude: implicit request for missing details such as dates, guest count, self-drive status, luggage load, elder or child travelers, or room preference
conservative wording around room availability, breakfast inclusion, and access convenience
guidance that helps the user clarify priorities instead of forcing a single exact recommendation
mustAvoid: claiming any room will fit equally well
guaranteeing easy arrival before confirming transport details
oversimplifying room differences into one generic inn pitch

cases.label: edge_case

input.strategy_route: Promote an atmospheric old-house inn with strong courtyard charm but structural boundaries around stairs, street sound, and room differences that must be handled carefully
input.offer_info: renovated old-house character, courtyard and tea-space appeal, some rooms near street or water, some upper-floor access by stairs only, natural sound varies by room and date, no zero-noise promise
input.audience_segment: image-driven travelers comparing local inns online but sensitive to mismatch between visuals and real stay experience

mustInclude: atmosphere-led positioning with clear boundaries around stairs, room type, sound exposure, and old-house structure
trust-building details that rely on real stay texture instead of perfect-photo fantasy
conservative treatment of quietness, accessibility, and room differences as both appeal and boundary
mustAvoid: pretending every room experience is identical
overclaiming privacy or visual proof that the property cannot fully support

cases.label: failure_case

input.strategy_route: Push fast conversion for an inn using the best inn in the old town language, every corner is photo-perfect claims, and hard scarcity around upcoming weekends
input.offer_info: small property, pressure to fill near-term dates, temptation to exaggerate atmosphere, views, and urgency, incomplete traveler details
input.audience_segment: cold leisure traffic casually comparing old-town stays for an upcoming short trip

mustInclude: explicit identification of risky ranking, photo-perfect, and scarcity phrases in risk_phrases
reframing urgency into date-confirmation and room-type confirmation guidance
trust built through real arrival, room difference, and local-stay texture instead of hype
mustAvoid: best inn in the old town claim without evidence

cases.label: failure_case

input.strategy_route: Force immediate booking for an inn by promising absolute privacy, zero noise, door-to-door car access, guaranteed views, 24-hour butler help, and the lowest rate for every date
input.offer_info: small inn in lanes or scenic area, uncertain occupancy, limited staffing, room differences by floor and orientation, desire to overstate convenience and service outcomes
input.audience_segment: mixed cold leads making quick comparisons across inns, homestays, and design hotels

mustInclude: clear conservative rewrite of privacy, quietness, access, service, view, and pricing promises
explicit marking of red-flag expressions in risk_phrases
CTA focused on dates, guest count, transport method, luggage or elder-child needs, room preference, and stay purpose before confirming options
